common.tagline: Bring your scattered music world back together.
common.disclaimer: An unofficial introduction and user guide. Not affiliated with Apple Inc.
common.notFound: Sorry, we couldn't find that page.

meta.title: TuneSync — Bring Your Own Music World Back
meta.description: Connect WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP servers and local folders, auto-fill metadata and artwork, enjoy synced lyrics, AI recommendations, and on-device vocal-separation karaoke.

home.badge: A native iOS music app built for power users
home.headline: Bring <accent>your own</accent> music world back
home.subhead: TuneSync connects servers, folders, and playback entry points into a more natural path: link WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP, organize your library, follow synced lyrics, let AI pick the next song, then dive into on-device karaoke.
home.downloadSubtitle: Start on iPhone today and bring your music servers, local library, and player into one place. More platforms are in the works.
home.downloadIOSAria: Open the iOS getting started guide

ios.label: iOS
ios.desc: A native iPhone experience for connecting servers, organizing your library, and immersive playback.

android.label: Android
android.desc: Android is on the roadmap, with the same complete music management experience planned.

macos.label: macOS
macos.desc: A desktop experience is coming later, bringing your music world to a larger screen.

windows.label: Windows
windows.desc: Windows is not available yet, but more listening and organizing workflows are planned.

home.showcasePlayerTitle: Line-by-line synced lyrics, scrolling with the beat
home.showcasePlayerDesc: The full-screen player fills the backdrop with a gradient extracted from the artwork. Lyrics support karaoke-style line highlighting, tap-to-seek, and drag-to-browse, with a one-tap reading mode.
home.showcasePlayerB2: Queue, sleep timer, AirPlay, loop, and speed
home.showcaseKaraokeTitle: Vocal separation + mic recording — karaoke at home
home.showcaseKaraokeDesc: An on-device AI model splits a song into vocal and accompaniment stems. Lower the original vocal, sing along, and record your voice mixed with the backing — with latency compensation to keep everything in sync.
home.showcaseKaraokeB1: On-device Core ML separation, results cached for reuse
home.showcaseKaraokeB2: Live vocal-level control with a 3-2-1 countdown
home.showcaseKaraokeB3: Saves to “My Recordings” for playback and sharing
home.showcaseAITitle: Bring your own key — plug in your own AI
home.showcaseAIDesc: Plug in your own API key and model (any OpenAI-compatible third-party AI), with separate instant / reasoning roles. The AI DJ auto-continues your queue, and tool calling lets the model search the web, fetch album art and lyrics, even control playback and playlists.
home.showcaseAIB1: Any OpenAI-compatible third-party AI — 40+ provider presets built in, or a custom endpoint
home.showcaseAIB2: Instant models for recommendations, reasoning models for album intros
home.showcaseAIB3: AI DJ with three autonomy levels: preview / one-tap / auto-continue
home.showcaseAIB4: 7+ built-in tools + custom tools, with tool chaining

dual.title: Instant + reasoning models
dual.desc: Instant models are fast and frugal — they handle recommendations and filename cleanup. Reasoning models go deeper, writing album intros and other complex tasks. Picked automatically per task, with adjustable reasoning effort.

dj.title: AI DJ continues your queue
dj.desc: Builds a playback queue from your listening profile with three autonomy levels: preview suggestions, one-tap confirm, and auto-continue when the queue runs low — the more you listen, the better it knows you.

tools.title: Tool calling — the model takes action
tools.desc: The model can search the web, fetch and upscale album art, and look up lyrics. Once authorized, it can even control playback and manage playlists. Supports custom tools and chaining.

home.pillarsTitle: Every step of listening, done right
home.pillarsSubtitle: From connecting your library to immersive playback, from smart organizing to on-device karaoke — TuneSync makes each step feel natural.

connect.title: Many sources, one library
connect.desc: Connect WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP servers or import local folders, auto-discover LAN devices via Bonjour, and index only the folders you actually want.

playback.title: An immersive player
playback.desc: The full-screen player is backdropped by a gradient from your artwork, with synced lyrics, queue, sleep timer, and AirPlay built in.

karaoke.title: On-device karaoke
karaoke.desc: Core ML splits vocal and accompaniment stems locally — lower the original vocal, sing along, and record with latency-compensated mixing.

ai.title: Plug in your own AI
ai.desc: Use your own API key with any OpenAI-compatible third-party AI (40+ presets built in), with instant / reasoning roles picked per task. AI DJ continues your queue, and tool calls let it search, fetch art, and control playback.

home.differentTitle: Your key, your device, your servers
home.differentSubtitle: TuneSync never locks in your music or calls AI on your behalf — control stays with you.

byok.title: Bring your own key
byok.desc: AI requests go straight to the provider you choose, on your own key and quota — no middleman.

ondevice.title: On-device processing
ondevice.desc: Vocal separation and lyric alignment run on local Core ML, with results cached for reuse. Your audio never leaves the device.

protocols.title: Open protocols
protocols.desc: WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP / DAAP plus Bonjour auto-discovery — connect the NAS and self-hosted servers you already have.

open.title: No lock-in
open.desc: What you connect, add, and organize is yours. No account wall, no hosting your files for you.

quote1.text: After loading my entire NAS library onto my phone, I finally stopped juggling five apps. Covers, lyrics, synced playback — all in one place.
quote1.author: Lin Yifan

quote2.text: The AI DJ’s auto-continue really gets me. When the queue runs low it always picks the right next song — no more stopping to choose.
quote2.author: M. Chen

quote3.text: On-device vocal-separation karaoke was a surprise. Lower the vocal, sing along, then replay to compare — practice at home.
quote3.author: Kai

quote4.text: My messy library got tidied up by multi-source metadata — covers, years, track numbers all filled in at once. Highly satisfying.
quote4.author: Zhang Rui

quote5.text: Bringing my own API key is exactly right. Requests go straight to my provider — no middleman. Privacy and cost stay in my hands.
quote5.author: D. Wang

quote6.text: Bonjour discovered my LAN media server instantly, and discovery-to-connection was seamless. The setup experience exceeded expectations.
quote6.author: Eric

home.ctaBody: Follow the quick-start guide to connect your music server in minutes and open up your music world.

features.title: One app, the whole listening flow
features.subtitle: From connecting your library to immersive playback, from smart organizing to on-device karaoke — TuneSync gets every step right.

library.title: Multi-source library
library.desc: Connect WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP servers or import local folders, auto-indexed into one library.
library.featureTitle: Your entire NAS music library, on your phone
library.featureDesc: Bonjour auto-discovers media servers on your LAN — configure once and connect. Folder-level indexing only includes the content you actually want, and playback starts the moment indexing finishes.
library.featureB1: WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP / DAAP protocols
library.featureB3: Folder-level indexing — only what you choose
library.featureB4: Mix local folders and remote servers in one library

player.title: Immersive player
player.desc: Full-screen player with synced lyrics, queue, sleep timer, and AirPlay.
player.featureDesc: The full-screen player fills the backdrop with a gradient pulled from the artwork and switches between three modes: standard, lyrics, and queue. Lyrics support karaoke-style highlighting, tap-to-seek, drag-to-browse, and confidence hints during AI alignment.

karaoke.title: Vocal-separation karaoke
karaoke.desc: On-device AI splits vocals from accompaniment; lower the original, record your voice with latency compensation.
karaoke.featureTitle: Your own private concert, at home
karaoke.featureDesc: Enter karaoke mode and an on-device Core ML model splits the song into vocal and accompaniment stems. Lower the original vocal, sing along, and record the mix in real time. The first split takes a moment and is then cached for reuse.
karaoke.featureB2: Live vocal-level control with a 3-2-1 countdown
karaoke.featureB3: Mic recording with input/output latency compensation
karaoke.featureB4: Saves to “My Recordings” for playback and sharing

ai.title: AI assistant
ai.desc: BYOK into any OpenAI-compatible third-party AI, with instant / reasoning model roles, tool calling and AI DJ.
ai.featureTitle: Your API key, your AI DJ
ai.featureDesc: BYOK into any OpenAI-compatible third-party AI — with 40+ presets built in (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Zhipu, DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen), plus custom endpoints or local Ollama. Separate instant and reasoning model roles: instant models handle recommendations and cleanup, reasoning models write album intros.
ai.featureB2: Instant / reasoning model roles, auto-selected per task
ai.featureB3: AI DJ with three autonomy levels: preview / one-tap / auto-continue
ai.featureB4: Tool calling: web search, album art, lyrics, playback and playlist control

metadata.title: Auto metadata
metadata.desc: iTunes / MusicBrainz / Deezer / AI multi-source fill for artwork, lyrics, and track info.
metadata.featureTitle: Tame a messy library back into shape
metadata.featureDesc: Multi-source matching fills in artwork, lyrics, release info, track numbers — even BPM, mood and energy. Works at track, album, and artist granularity, supports full-library batch refresh, and source weights decide who to trust.
metadata.featureB1: iTunes / MusicBrainz / Deezer / AI multi-source matching
metadata.featureB2: Track / album / artist refresh + batch organizing

discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.desc: Aggregates Apple Music / Bilibili / YouTube / Last.fm charts, with ShazamKit recognition.
discovery.featureTitle: Not sure what to play? Let charts and AI inspire you
discovery.featureDesc: The home tab aggregates ranked charts across platforms and generates a “For You” recommendation feed from your listening profile. Hear something great? Identify it in one tap with ShazamKit, with history kept automatically.
discovery.featureB1: Apple Music / Bilibili / YouTube / Last.fm multi-source charts
discovery.featureB2: “For You” smart recommendations from your listening profile

pricing.title: Core listening is free; advanced power is VIP
pricing.subtitle: Listening, library, and lyrics stay free; vocal separation, batch organizing, and discovery tools appear as VIP capabilities. This is a pre-launch capability preview and does not block these features.

free.title: Free
free.subtitle: A complete local and remote listening experience
benefits: Import local folders, connect WebDAV or FTP servers, and build indexes
Browse your library by songs, albums, artists, favorites, and playlists
Play queue, shuffle, repeat, volume, and progress controls
View lyrics, import LRC files, edit covers and song information
Manage cache space, appearance, language, and basic playback preferences

vip.title: TuneSync VIP
vip.subtitle: Unlock high-cost processing, automated organizing, and discovery tools
benefits: Vocal separation and karaoke: generate accompaniment and vocal stems, enter karaoke playback, tune vocal retention
Batch metadata organizing: batch-scrape covers, lyrics, and song info, with full-library refresh
Song recognition: use ShazamKit to identify nearby music and keep recent recognition history
Multi-source trend discovery: aggregate Apple Music, Bilibili, YouTube, and Last.fm charts on Home
Advanced lyrics tuning: adjust lyric font size, motion, focus position, and line animations
Background task center: view separation, scraping, queue, and cache tasks, plus early access to future tools

pricing.note: This page only previews membership benefits before launch. It does not block vocal separation, song recognition, or batch organizing.

intro.title: TuneSync documentation
intro.lead: Start here to understand every feature and how to use it.
intro.body: This documentation follows the real usage flow: from connecting your first server and organizing the library, to immersive playback, synced lyrics, on-device karaoke, the AI assistant, and metadata organizing. Each page has steps and tips — come back any time you need a reference.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.lead: First time opening TuneSync? Follow the four-step guide to connect your music world.
gettingStarted.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/getting-started.png

overview.title: The overall flow
overview.body: On first launch, TuneSync walks through four guide pages — welcome, connect servers, add library, and polish (artwork, lyrics, karaoke) — then enters a setup wizard that tracks each step as a checklist.

step1.title: Welcome
step1.body: “Bring Your Own Music World Back” — how TuneSync connects servers, folders, and playback entry points into one natural path.

step2.title: Connect servers
step2.body: Supports WebDAV, SMB, NFS, and FTP. Connect a server first, then expand your library. Manage server status and test connections in one place — discovery to connection in a single flow.

step3.title: Add your library
step3.body: Add only the folders you really want. Playback starts after indexing, and TuneSync begins fetching artwork and lyrics for that content.

step4.title: Polish
step4.body: Artwork, lyrics, and karaoke: tap a karaoke-ready track to enter sing mode with auto-shown lyrics, live vocal-level adjustment, and lock-screen / background playback.

tip.title: Revisit any time
tip.body: Reopen the guide under “Settings → User Guide”, or track your progress as a checklist under “Settings → Setup Guide”.

permissions.title: Permissions you’ll be asked for
permissions.body: When you first use a relevant feature, the app requests these permissions on demand:
permissions.localNetwork: Local network — discover and connect to LAN WebDAV / FTP / SMB / NFS servers
permissions.microphone: Microphone — song recognition and karaoke recording
permissions.speech: Speech recognition — on-device transcription to generate time-aligned synced lyrics
permissions.applemusic: Apple Music — supports background audio playback

next.title: Next up
next.body: Ready to connect a server? Continue to “Library”.

library.title: Library
library.lead: Combine local folders and remote servers into one unified library.
library.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/library.png

protocols.title: Supported protocols
protocols.body: TuneSync connects to self-hosted or NAS media servers over these protocols:
protocols.webdav: The most common self-hosted option; supports encrypted WebDAVs
protocols.smb: Windows sharing and most NAS devices, enabled by default
protocols.nfs: Common network filesystem on Linux / NAS
protocols.ftp: Traditional file transfer; supports encrypted FTPS

step1.title: Open library sources
step1.body: Under “Settings → Library Sources & Indexing”, tap “Add Server”, or enter from the Home “Connect a Server” guide.

step2.title: Pick a protocol and address
step2.body: Choose WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP, then enter the server address, port, and share path. Use “Test Connection” to verify reachability.

step3.title: Enter credentials and save
step3.body: Enter username and password if needed. After saving, the server appears in the list as connected.

step4.title: Choose folders to index
step4.body: Browse the server and check only the folders you actually want. Playback is ready once indexing finishes.

discover.title: Auto-discover with Bonjour
discover.body: The app auto-discovers LAN services like _webdav._tcp, _smb._tcp, _ftp._tcp, _nfs._tcp via Bonjour; discovered servers go straight into connection setup, no manual address needed.

indexing.title: Indexing and scanning
indexing.body: After adding a folder, the app scans audio files, reads embedded metadata, and builds the library structure. Playback starts once it’s done. Scanning runs in the background — track progress on Home and under “Settings → Background Tasks & Logs”.
indexing.note: Only the folders you choose are indexed, so unrelated disk content never ends up in your library.

playlists.title: Playlists and favorites
playlists.body: On the “Albums & Playlists” tab you can:
playlists.add: Add any track to a playlist
playlists.favorite: Favorite a track — favorites surface on Home and in the favorites list

player.title: Player
player.lead: Full-screen player, mini-player, queue, and playback controls.
player.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/player-lyrics.png

modes.title: Three content modes
modes.body: The full-screen player switches between three modes via the bottom control area:
modes.normal: Standard: large artwork, track info, scrubber, and controls

controls.title: Playback controls
controls.scrub: Drag the scrubber to seek precisely

menu.title: Top menu
menu.body: The full-screen player’s top menu provides:

queue.title: Play queue
queue.body: The “Play Queue” shows “Up Next” tracks — tap to play, swipe to remove, or clear all (with confirmation). Empty states prompt you to add songs from your library.

bg.title: Background and lock-screen playback
bg.body: The app declares a background audio entitlement, so playback continues on the lock screen and in the background — Control Center and the lock screen both control it.

lyrics.title: Lyrics
lyrics.lead: Line-by-line synced lyrics, reading mode, and multi-source lyrics.
lyrics.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/lyrics.png

modes.title: Two display modes
modes.body: The lyrics view switches automatically based on whether a timeline exists:
modes.synced: Synced: aligned to playback, karaoke-style highlighting, auto-scroll, tap-to-seek, drag-to-browse (auto-follow resumes after release)
modes.reading: Reading mode: plain text lyrics, no timeline

sources.title: Lyrics sources
sources.body: TuneSync tries these sources in priority order:
sources.lrclib: Open synced-lyrics database — exact match first, then search
sources.genius: Search and parse from page HTML

webdav.v: a .lrc / .txt beside the audio downloaded over WebDAV

import.title: Import LRC
import.body: From the player’s top menu, choose “Import LRC” and pick a lyrics file. It’s parsed into a timeline and takes effect immediately.

generate.title: Generate synced lyrics
generate.body: If you only have plain-text lyrics, choose “Generate Synced Lyrics” from the top menu. The app transcribes the vocal on-device, aligns it with the text to produce a timeline, and shows confidence hints along the way.

confidence.title: About confidence
confidence.body: AI-aligned lyrics include a confidence score; low-confidence results show a warning and support retry. Results are cached on the track to avoid recomputation.

karaoke.title: Karaoke
karaoke.lead: On-device vocal separation + mic recording — your own private concert.
karaoke.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/karaoke.png

how.title: How it works
how.body: Enter karaoke mode and an on-device Core ML model splits the song into vocal and accompaniment stems. A dual-track mixing engine adjusts each stem independently: lower the original vocal, keep the accompaniment, and sing along.

step1.title: Prepare vocal separation
step1.body: Tap the mic button in the player and choose “Prepare Karaoke”. An on-device Core ML model splits the song into vocal and accompaniment stems, processing in chunks with results cached for reuse.

step2.title: Start karaoke
step2.body: Once ready, the button becomes “Start Karaoke”. Tap it to enter dual-track mixing: the accompaniment plays while the vocal stem is lowered.

step3.title: Adjust vocals and record
step3.body: Use the vertical vocal slider to set the original vocal level. Tap the record button when ready — after a 3-2-1 countdown, recording captures your voice mixed with the accompaniment.

step4.title: Save and replay
step4.body: On stop, choose “Save and Stop”, “Discard Recording”, or “Continue Recording”. Saved recordings appear under “Settings → My Recordings” for playback and sharing.

balance.title: Adjust the vocal stem
balance.body: The karaoke console has a vertical vocal slider: swipe up to raise the original vocal, push to the top to collapse it back into a mic button. After separation completes it auto-expands with haptic feedback.

record.title: Microphone recording
record.body: Tap the record button to start the recording flow:
record.meter: Live input level meter (green → yellow → red) and timer while recording
record.save: On stop, choose “Save and Stop”, “Discard Recording”, or “Continue Recording”

model.title: First separation needs a model
model.body: Vocal separation relies on an on-device Core ML model (MDX-light). First use requires preparing the model under “Settings → Vocal Separation”; separation runs in chunks with overlap-add, and results are cached for reuse — the first run is costlier.

recordings.title: My Recordings
recordings.body: Recordings are saved as SingRecording items — play, share, and manage their storage under “Settings → My Recordings”.

live.title: Live Activity
live.body: Separation tasks support Live Activity, showing progress in the Dynamic Island and on the lock screen.

ai.title: AI assistant
ai.lead: Bring your own API key to recommend, organize, and write intros.
ai.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/ai-dj.png

step1.title: Open AI assistance
step1.body: Turn on “AI Assistance” under “Settings → Metadata & Lyrics → AI Assistance”.

step2.title: Pick a provider
step2.body: Under “Connection”, choose a provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Zhipu, DeepSeek and 40+ presets, or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint) and enter the Base URL.

step3.title: Enter API key and model
step3.body: Enter your API key, then pick an instant model and a reasoning model (type a custom name or choose from “Available Models”).

step4.title: Test the connection
step4.body: Tap “Test Connection” to verify, or use “AI Trial Chat” to send a message and confirm the AI is ready.

roles.title: Dual model roles
roles.body: TuneSync distinguishes two model roles, auto-selected per task:

instant.v: Fast and low-cost — AI DJ recommendations, filename cleanup, etc.

reasoning.v: Deep and high-quality — album intros and complex tasks; reasoning effort is configurable

dj.title: AI DJ
dj.body: The AI DJ builds a play queue from your listening profile, with three autonomy levels:
dj.preview: Preview before play: surface recommendations for review
dj.oneTap: One-tap play: play immediately after confirmation
dj.auto: Automatic continuation: auto-add songs as the queue runs low

tools.title: Tool calling
tools.body: With tool calling enabled, the model can use these tools (some permission-gated):
tools.library: search_library: search within your local library

privacy.title: About privacy
privacy.body: AI calls use your own API key and go directly to your chosen provider. Enable tool calling and “smart tool selection” as needed — keep capability while controlling cost.

trial.title: Trial chat
trial.body: Under “AI Assistance” there’s a trial chat to verify your connection works without entering a specific feature.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.lead: Multi-source matching fills in artwork, lyrics, and track info to tidy your library.
metadata.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/metadata.png

sources.title: Metadata sources
sources.body: TuneSync pulls metadata from multiple sources and picks the best match:
sources.itunes: Artwork, release info, album tracklist; artwork upscalable to 1200×1200
sources.ai: AI cleanup of filenames and inference of missing fields

embedded.v: tags stored in the audio file itself

single.title: Single-track refresh
single.body: From track details or the player’s top menu, choose “Search Metadata”. The app lists candidates scored by match quality and writes back your selection.

album.title: Album refresh
album.body: The album detail page supports batch refresh of an entire album:
album.intro: Generate an AI album intro (web-verified, streaming preview)

artist.title: Artist refresh
artist.body: The artist detail page refreshes artist info — biography, image, MusicBrainz / Wikidata / Wikipedia IDs — and applies it across that artist’s tracks.

weights.title: Source weights
weights.body: Under “Settings → Metadata & Lyrics” you can configure source weights and per-field “overwrite” policies, deciding who to trust when sources conflict and whether to overwrite existing data.

batch.title: Batch organizing
batch.body: Full-library batch refresh is supported; the background task center tracks progress. Apply strategies include “standard”, “metadata only”, and “batch disk artwork”.

history.title: History and rollback
history.body: Every refresh records per-field sources and snapshots for traceability and rollback.

discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.lead: Home recommendations, multi-source charts, and song recognition to find your next track.
discovery.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/discovery.png

home.title: Home recommendations
home.body: Home dynamically shows sections based on your library:
home.foryou: For You: smart recommendation playlists from your listening profile

trends.title: Multi-source charts
trends.body: After enabling chart sources under “Settings → Home & Discovery”, Home shows ranked charts from:

foryou.title: For You recommendations
foryou.body: For You generates a primary recommendation card (a multi-track artwork collage) plus secondary picks, each with a reason and a “Preview Mix” button that opens a preview sheet.

feedback.title: Feedback tuning
feedback.body: Not loving a recommendation? Give the AI DJ feedback (like / less of this) from the player menu — TuneSync adjusts future recommendations accordingly.

recognize.title: Song recognition
recognize.body: Identify music playing around you with ShazamKit; recognition history is kept and browsable under “Settings → Song Recognition”.

settings.title: Settings
settings.lead: Appearance, language, player animation, cache, and background tasks.
settings.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/settings.png

cache.title: Cache and storage
cache.body: Under “Settings → Cache & Storage”, review and clear artwork, audio, and temp cache usage.

tasks.title: Background tasks and logs
tasks.body: “Settings → Background Tasks & Logs” centralizes progress, cancellable tasks, and failure reasons for four background task types:

guide.title: User guide
guide.body: “Settings → User Guide” reopens the first-launch guide; “Settings → Setup Guide” tracks your progress as a checklist.

about.title: About
about.body: “Settings → About TuneSync” shows version, vocal-separation model status, and third-party credits.
